entry.title: Week 27, 2026: Release v2.15.0 shipped with per-operation RWF flags and a liburing 2.15 update, plus fixes for opam sandbox builds and Eio compatibility.
entry.content:
Summary

Three PRs opened and merged within the week for build sandboxing and FLAGS.t compatibility, alongside the RWF flags work and the v2.15.0 release.

Activity

  • All three PRs opened this week were merged the same week: the liburing 2.15 update by Anil Madhavapeddy (#157), followed in quick succession by Thomas Leonard's opam-sandbox build fix (#158) and FLAGS.t compatibility revert (#159), both merged within a day of opening.
  • The per-operation RWF flags PR opened in the prior week by Anil Madhavapeddy was merged this week (#154), rounding out the API work that fed into the v2.15.0 release published by Anil Madhavapeddy.
  • Discussion continued on the open issue about building without vendored liburing, with Anil Madhavapeddy explaining the opam-distribution rationale and exploring an optional environment-variable toggle for a future version (#156).
entry.summary: Release v2.15.0 shipped with per-operation RWF flags and a liburing 2.15 update, plus fixes for opam sandbox builds and Eio compatibility.

entry.title: Week 24, 2026: Socket creation now defaults to CLOEXEC; new PRs add RWF flags and vectored I/O bindings to ocaml-uring.
entry.content:
Summary

Three new PRs opened for low-level I/O improvements; two older experimental PRs closed; bytes buffer migration ongoing.

Activity

  • New PR #155 opened by Anil Madhavapeddy: adds correct vectored read/writev bindings for fixed buffers, fixing previously misnamed C stubs that were not performing vectored calls (172 additions, 20 deletions across 5 files).
  • New PR #154 opened by Anil Madhavapeddy: exposes preadv2/pwritev2 RWF flags at the uring layer, enabling callers like eio_linux to use RWF_DSYNC and RWF_NOCACHE to reduce unnecessary kernel operations.
  • Draft PR #153 switching the buffer type from bigarray/cstruct to bytes is active with ongoing benchmarking: Anil Madhavapeddy shared microbenchmark results showing no regression in filesystem I/O throughput, improved large-buffer allocation (avoids bigarray finalizer overhead), but worse small-buffer performance due to a 2KB minimum allocation floor needed to keep bytes immovable.
  • PR #151 merged by Anil Madhavapeddy: socket flags support landed with CLOEXEC as the new default, enabling ocaml-multicore/eio#843 to use uring socket creation directly.
  • PR #152 (Linux errno module) closed without merging: Thomas Leonard noted the module has limited utility in Eio since Unix_error is always required, and suggested adding useful errnos upstream to OCaml instead. Discussion referenced ocaml-multicore/eio#788 and ocaml-multicore/eio#851.
  • PR #101 (Cstruct-to-Bstruct experiment) closed by Patrick Ferris in favour of the more comprehensive bytes migration in #153.
entry.summary: Socket creation now defaults to CLOEXEC; new PRs add RWF flags and vectored I/O bindings to ocaml-uring.

entry.title: Week 23, 2026: Busy week adding fallocate, ftruncate, shutdown, socket, renameat, symlinkat bindings and fixing Statx API types.
entry.content:
Summary

Three PRs remain open including an errno module, a bytes-based buffer redesign, and socket-flag support; eight PRs merged.

Activity

  • Newly opened this week and still open: an errno module adding Linux-specific error codes by Anil Madhavapeddy (#152); a draft PR switching the buffer type from bigarray/cstruct to bytes (surprisingly small diff) (#153); and a PR to allow setting CLOEXEC on socket creation, motivated by ocaml-multicore/eio#843, with discussion about whether this should be always-on (#151)
  • Filesystem operations expanded: fallocate and ftruncate bindings added with kernel-version guards in tests for the very new FALLOC_FL_WRITE_ZEROES flag (#149); renameat with rename flags hard-coded for older musl compatibility (#147); symlinkat with corrected symlink-follow test (#147)
  • Networking operations expanded with shutdown and async socket creation bindings, reusing the OCaml runtime's socket table rather than redefining it (#147)
  • Null CQE safety check added to caml_uring_wait_cqe as a C assert, matching the existing check in the timeout variant (#148)
  • Debug-mode build warnings fixed: corrected %p format specifiers and errno sign in ocaml_uring_unregister_buffers, and additional URING_DEBUG format warnings across uring_stubs.c (#146)
  • Minimum OCaml version raised to 5.2, removing a now-unnecessary compiler warning (#150)
  • lintcstubs-arity depopt added to the test dependency to ensure primitives.h regeneration is caught in CI (#143)
entry.summary: Busy week adding fallocate, ftruncate, shutdown, socket, renameat, symlinkat bindings and fixing Statx API types.

entry.title: Week 22, 2026: v2.14.0 released; AT_FDCWD handling moved to C eliminating Obj.magic; several bug fixes and new op PRs opened.
entry.content:
Summary

Several PRs opened for new iouring ops and fixes; lintcstubs-arity CI dependency corrected; iouring_submit error reporting improved.

Activity

  • New ops PR opened by Anil Madhavapeddy adding shutdown, socket, renameat, and symlinkat operations with individual commits and tests, fixing the symlink-follow test for current liburing (#147) — not yet merged.
  • Statx Attr API bug found and PR opened by Anil Madhavapeddy: the supported check had an inverted condition, uncovered by new tests (#142) — not yet merged.
  • Region.init bounds check added to raise early on invalid parameters rather than failing on subsequent access, opened by Anil Madhavapeddy (#145) — opened this week, merged the following week.
  • io_uring_submit error reporting fixed by Thomas Leonard: previously a negative return value was silently passed through; now raises a Unix error (#144) — opened this week, merged the following week.
  • Debug message fixes in ocaml_uring_unregister_buffers correcting missing %p format args and wrong sign for errno, opened by Anil Madhavapeddy (#146) — opened this week, merged the following week.
  • lintcstubs-arity dependency changed from depopt to hard test dependency so CI reliably catches missing primitive regenerations; primitives header regenerated (#143).
entry.summary: v2.14.0 released; AT_FDCWD handling moved to C eliminating Obj.magic; several bug fixes and new op PRs opened.

entry.title: Week 21, 2026: liburing updated to 2.14, new zero-copy and io_uring ops exposed, GC registration bug fixed.
entry.content:
Summary

All four PRs opened this week were merged: GC bug fix, build docs, liburing 2.14 update, and new zero-copy ops.

Activity

  • GC safety fix for ocaml_uring_setup landed same-day after Vesa Karvonen reported that the polling_timeout parameter was missing from CAMLparam registration, merged by Thomas Leonard (#136, closes #135)
  • Build process documentation and simplification merged by Thomas Leonard, with a note that the subtree scheme was previously broken and a follow-up update was already planned (#137)
  • Vendored liburing updated to 2.14 in a large squashed subtree merge, followed immediately by Thomas Leonard adding new operation and flags enums to the OCaml wrappers (#138)
  • Missing send_zc and sendmsg_zc ops added to discover.ml by Anil Madhavapeddy, which were absent from the capability discovery list despite being present in liburing (#139)
entry.summary: liburing updated to 2.14, new zero-copy and io_uring ops exposed, GC registration bug fixed.

entry.title: Week 20, 2026: New Uring.Res module for type-safe result handling, CLOEXEC fix for recvmsg FDs, and ZFS write_fixed warning added.
entry.content:
Summary

Four PRs merged covering the new Res API, a CLOEXEC bug fix, ZFS documentation, and a test hang fix; two long-standing draft PRs closed.

Activity

  • Uring.Res module merged by Thomas Leonard, drawing on earlier prototype work by David Allsopp in #129 and #130; the adopted approach uses a private int to avoid extra C call overhead while still providing type-safe fd and error conversions (#131)
  • CLOEXEC bug fix for recvmsg-based FD passing merged by Thomas Leonard, addressing a race condition in multi-threaded programs where a child process could inherit FDs before the flag was set manually (#132)
  • ZFS warning documentation added to write_fixed API, prompted by a suggestion from @toastal, closing the long-standing ZFS spin-hang bug report (#133, closes #113)
  • Test hang fix on Linux 5.10 merged: when the sending side of a test fails, the test no longer waits for a receive that will never arrive (#134)
  • Two draft PRs closed without merging: David Allsopp's #129 (fd-from-int via extra C call) and #130 (fully typed wait results encoding type in job ID), both superseded by the lighter-weight approach in #131
  • Three old issues closed: WSL2 sendmsg failure #55 closed as presumed resolved by WSL kernel updates; Uring.accept unusable FD #117 closed as fixed by #131; ZFS write hang #113 closed with documentation warning in #133
entry.summary: New Uring.Res module for type-safe result handling, CLOEXEC fix for recvmsg FDs, and ZFS write_fixed warning added.

entry.title: Week 18, 2026: A draft PR from 2023 to fix Linux 5.x kernel header imports was closed without merging due to conflicts.
entry.content:
Summary

Draft PR #110 addressing Linux 5.x kernel fcntl.h header compatibility was closed unmerged after sitting open since 2023.

Activity

  • A long-standing draft PR from Chris Armstrong proposing to import fnctl.h from linux/fcntl.h to access io_uring constants only available in Linux 5.x series kernels was closed without merging (#110); the PR had accumulated merge conflicts and remained in draft state since October 2023.
entry.summary: A draft PR from 2023 to fix Linux 5.x kernel header imports was closed without merging due to conflicts.

entry.title: Week 41, 2025: Design discussions on file descriptor representation and type safety in the OCaml bindings
entry.content:
Summary

Two open PRs exploring file descriptor type safety with active design discussions

Activity

  • David Allsopp opened two PRs exploring type-safe file descriptor handling in the library: #128 eliminates Obj.magic by handling AT_FDCWD in C stubs, while draft #130 proposes more invasive changes to introduce type-safe wait functions that differentiate between different return types
  • Extended discussion on #128 involving Thomas Leonard and Anil Madhavapeddy debated the abstraction boundaries between OCaml and C representations of file descriptors, with references to David's blog post on File descriptors are not integers and upstream OCaml PR ocaml/ocaml#14258
  • Anil Madhavapeddy indicated readiness to merge #128 pending end-to-end testing with Eio, while discussion continued on optimal approaches for flambda optimization of optional arguments and potential deeper integration into the Eio Linux backend
entry.summary: Design discussions on file descriptor representation and type safety in the OCaml bindings

entry.title: Week 40, 2025: Three PRs opened addressing type safety and eliminating Obj.magic in file descriptor handling
entry.content:
Summary

Type safety improvements for file descriptor handling with alternative approaches under discussion

Activity

  • Three new PRs opened this week by David Allsopp focusing on type safety and eliminating unsafe code: type-safe wait functions (#130), safer file descriptor wizardry (#129), and handling AT_FDCWD in C to eliminate Obj.magic (#128)
  • David Allsopp proposed type-safe wait functions as a more invasive alternative to address issue #117, ensuring OCaml ints only represent actual integers rather than encoded values, with no allocation difference for the fast path and no cost for retrieving Unix.file_descr values from syscalls returning descriptors (#130)
  • Design discussion emerged around file descriptor abstraction between David Allsopp and Anil Madhavapeddy exploring whether file descriptors should be treated as abstract types rather than integers, with Thomas Leonard noting similarities to Eio's existing approach in ocaml-multicore/eio (#130)
  • Alternative approach in #129 provides simpler solution to #117 at the cost of an extra C call, currently on hold pending resolution of design discussion in #130
entry.summary: Three PRs opened addressing type safety and eliminating Obj.magic in file descriptor handling

entry.title: Week 39, 2025: Major v2.7.0 release with liburing 2.7 upgrade, new socket operations, and race condition fixes
entry.content:
Summary

Release preparation and bug fixes culminating in v2.7.0 with extensive security and functionality improvements

Activity

  • Version 2.7.0 release completed by Anil Madhavapeddy including liburing upgrade, socket operations, and comprehensive documentation improvements (#126)
  • Socket operations implementation merged by Anil Madhavapeddy after extensive testing with Eio test suite and manual verification (#118)
  • Critical data race fix implemented by Anil Madhavapeddy addressing completion queue entry use-after-free issue identified by Vesa Karvonen (#124, #123)
  • Test infrastructure improvements by Thomas Leonard fixing mkdir test compatibility issues for opam repository integration (#127)
entry.summary: Major v2.7.0 release with liburing 2.7 upgrade, new socket operations, and race condition fixes

entry.title: Week 36, 2025: Three PRs merged adding Setup_flags API, cleaning up configurator code, and introducing mkdirat function
entry.content:
Summary

Configurator cleanup merged improving code maintainability with updated primitives header

Activity

  • Configurator code modernization by Thomas Leonard cleaning up the build configuration and updating primitives.h for easier future extensions (#121)
entry.summary: Three PRs merged adding Setup_flags API, cleaning up configurator code, and introducing mkdirat function

entry.title: Week 34, 2025: New mkdirat API function added to Uring with directory creation support
entry.content:
Summary

Single PR opened for mkdirat functionality implementation

Activity

  • New PR opened by Patrick Ferris implementing mkdirat API function (#120) with 48 additions across 4 files including ML interface, C bindings, and test coverage
entry.summary: New mkdirat API function added to Uring with directory creation support

entry.title: Week 29, 2025: Issue discussion about ZFS compatibility with fixed buffers received upstream fix information
entry.content:
Summary

Issue discussion about ZFS hanging issue provided information about upstream fix

Activity

  • Issue discussion about ZFS compatibility problem where Thomas Leonard received information about upstream fix for hanging with fixed buffers issue (#113)
entry.summary: Issue discussion about ZFS compatibility with fixed buffers received upstream fix information

entry.title: Week 48, 2024: Issue #119 debugging hang in _io_uring_get_cqe resolved as not a bug in ocaml-uring
entry.content:
Summary

Single issue closure involving debugging assistance for io_uring hang

Activity

  • Issue debugging session where Krzysztof Leśniak reported a hang in _io_uring_get_cqe and Thomas Leonard provided troubleshooting guidance and closed the issue (#119)
entry.summary: Issue #119 debugging hang in _io_uring_get_cqe resolved as not a bug in ocaml-uring

entry.title: Week 47, 2024: Issue reported about random hang in io_uring completion queue handling with debugging guidance provided
entry.content:
Summary

Single issue opened about io_uring hang with community debugging support

Activity

  • Krzysztof Leśniak reported a random hang issue in _io_uring_get_cqe affecting long-running programs using cohttp-eio and Eio.Time.sleep_until (#119)
  • Thomas Leonard provided debugging guidance with tracing recommendations using OCAMLRUNTIMEEVENTS for diagnosing the hang condition (#119)
entry.summary: Issue reported about random hang in io_uring completion queue handling with debugging guidance provided
